claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to know some information about your child or children.

**First question: How old is your child?**

This is important because eligibility depends on age:
- Children **under 16** may automatically qualify (with some conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** generally don't qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, let me know about each of them.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex, who's 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17 (which falls in the 16-19 age range), their eligibility will depend on whether they're in approved education or training.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question: Is Alex currently in education or training?**

For 16-19 year olds like Alex, Child Benefit can continue if they are:
- Currently enrolled in **approved education or training**, OR
- Have recently left education (within the last 20 weeks)

Which of these applies to Alex:
1. **Currently in education or training**
2. **Recently left education or training** (within the last 20 weeks)
3. **Neither** - not in education and didn't recently leave

Please let me know which situation applies to Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They left school a while back and aren't studying anywhere right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. So Alex left education a while back and isn't currently in any education or training.

Just to confirm - you mentioned Alex left school "a while back." Can you tell me approximately how long ago Alex left education? Specifically, was it:

- **Less than 20 weeks ago** (roughly 5 months or less), OR
- **More than 20 weeks ago**

This matters because there's a potential extension period of up to 20 weeks after leaving approved education or training, during which you might still be eligible for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, it's been more than that - probably closer to a year now, I'd say.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for confirming that. Since Alex left education about a year ago (which is more than 20 weeks), and isn't currently in any education or training, let me navigate to the outcome for this situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Result: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit** for Alex.

### Why this decision was made:

For children aged 16-19 like Alex (who is 17), they must be in **approved education or training** to qualify as a "qualifying child" for Child Benefit purposes. Since:

- Alex is not currently in education or training, AND
- Alex left education about a year ago (beyond the 20-week extension period)

Alex does not meet the qualifying child criteria at this time.

### Important information:

**If Alex returns to approved education or training**, you may become eligible for Child Benefit again. This could include:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or equivalent)
- Approved training programs
- Courses up to and including Level 3 (such as NVQ Level 3)

The education must be:
- At least 12 hours per week of supervised study or course-related work experience
- Provided by an eligible institution (school, college, or approved training provider)

### What you can do:

If Alex enrolls in approved education or training in the future, you can reapply for Child Benefit at that time. You would need to provide details of the course and the educational institution.

If you have any questions about what qualifies as approved education or training, you can contact HMRC's Child Benefit Office for more specific guidance.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which falls in the 16-19 age category. For children aged 16-19, they must be in approved education or training to qualify for Child Benefit. Alex is not currently in education or training. Alex left education approximately one year ago, which is well beyond the 20-week extension period that can apply after leaving approved education. Since Alex does not meet the requirement of being in approved education or training, and the extension period has expired, Alex does not qualify as a qualifying child for Child Benefit purposes.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 17. Since Alex is in the 16-19 age range but is not in approved education or training and left education over a year ago (beyond the 20-week extension period), Alex does not meet the qualifying child criteria. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.
